objective: Implement the packet queue and controlled orchestration slice from epic #224 by defining filesystem queue states, atomic packet movement, dispatch validation, PII halt behavior, and replayable audit records without executing packet payloads.
scope_boundary: Extend the governance packet schema compatibly with issue-backed dispatch metadata.
Implement a filesystem-first packet queue under raw/packets/queue with inbound, dispatched, review, gate, done, and halted states.
Validate packets before dispatch using existing SoM packet checks plus issue-backed structured plan approval.
Require model identity, validation commands, review artifacts, PII mode, and fallback log metadata before dispatch.
Record auditable, replayable state transitions and support dry-run movement without executing code.
out_of_scope: Executing packet payloads or spawning workers from the queue.
Starting an always-on daemon or launchd job for packet dispatch.
Changing local-ai-machine runtime code from this governance slice.
Sending packet contents to cloud or Windows model endpoints.
Granting autonomous write authority outside explicit approved issue-backed plans.
research_summary: Issue #229 requires deterministic packet queue semantics for epic #224. Existing packet validation in scripts/packet/validate.py already enforces schema shape, model family independence, no self-approval, planning floor, PII path checks, tier escalation, local family diversity, and fallback log resolution. This slice layers queue-state controls on top of that validator so existing packets remain compatible while dispatch-ready packets must carry explicit governance metadata. The read-only observer from #227 intentionally records packet_enqueue_enabled: false, and #228 keeps model runtime probing metadata-only, so #229 must remain dry-run/control-plane only.

sprints.name: Queue Contract
sprints.goal: Define packet queue metadata and governance-surface planning coverage.
tasks: Extend som-packet schema with optional governance dispatch metadata.
Classify packet queue paths as governance surface for issue-backed plan enforcement.
Document packet queue states and audit fields in registry docs.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch-ready packets can declare issue, plan, scope, validation, review, fallback, PII, and authorization metadata.
Packet queue files cannot change without a matching implementation-ready plan.

sprints.name: Queue State Machine
sprints.goal: Implement deterministic packet movement and dispatch checks.
tasks: Add scripts/orchestrator/packet_queue.py with queue initialization, transition, and replay commands.
Use atomic write-then-rename semantics for packet writes and state moves.
Require approved issue-backed structured plans before dispatch.
Halt or refuse PII-tagged packets before non-LAM dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: A dry-run packet can move through queue states without execution.
Invalid packets fail schema validation before dispatch.
PII packets cannot dispatch to cloud or Windows roles.
Audit logs replay into deterministic latest states.

sprints.name: Review And Closeout
sprints.goal: Complete reviewed evidence and closeout for issue #229.
tasks: Add PDCAR, cross-review, and closeout artifacts.
Run packet, orchestrator, planning gate, model pin, graph, and compendium validations.
Open, verify, and merge the issue-specific implementation PR.
acceptance_criteria: Alternate-family review is recorded before merge.
Validation commands are captured in closeout.
Compendium, graph, and wiki outputs are current after closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Filesystem state machine, dispatch validation, dry-run behavior, PII halt behavior, and audit replay.
specialist_reviews.summary: The slice adds queue control-plane validation and auditability only. It does not execute code or start autonomous dispatch.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review is required for Tier 1 orchestration and PII-routing controls. Findings are recorded in the #229 cross-review artifact and must be resolved or explicitly accepted before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement #229 packet queue schema metadata, dry-run state machine, dispatch gate, PII halt behavior, audit replay, tests, docs, review, and closeout only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Build queue controls, run validators, obtain alternate-family review, close out, open PR, and merge after checks pass.

material_deviation_rules: If dispatch validation would need to execute packet payloads, stop and defer execution to a later issue.
If packet metadata conflicts with the issue-backed structured plan, refuse dispatch.
If PII mode is tagged, detected, or LAM-only and the prior role is not a LAM role, halt or refuse before dispatch.
If existing packet schema compatibility would break, keep governance metadata optional and enforce stricter rules in the queue layer.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, loop through, no HITL
